title: pedicularis plicata subsp. luteola
intro: Pedicularis plicata subsp. luteola, also known as the yellow leafed lousewort, is a plant species within the Pedicularis genus, characterized by its unique yellow leaves and a cluster of small flowers. Traditionally, Pedicularis plicata subsp. luteola has been used for its medicinal properties, including its expectorant and antiseptic qualities, which help to relieve respiratory issues and promote wound healing. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Pedicularis plicata subsp. luteola is often used as a tea or tincture, or added to salves and ointments to take advantage of its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
